questions: Do the 3 new flags (--after, --reference, --implement-from) correctly resolve references and include them in the POST body?
Does the error handling correctly extract server validation errors from hxFetch em-dash-separated error messages?
Are the TicketDetail and TicketItem type extensions correct for the server response shapes?
Is the conditional display logic correct in get.ts and list.ts?
Are all documentation surfaces consistent with the actual CLI flag names and usage patterns?
Do quality gates pass after all changes?

answers.answer: Yes. All 3 flags are parsed with getFlag(), resolved via resolveTicket(config, ref) which returns { id, shortId }, and the resolved IDs are conditionally spread into the POST body. The --reference flag correctly splits on commas, validates max 5 client-side, and resolves each reference sequentially. The pattern exactly mirrors the existing --repos resolution pattern.
evidence: src/tickets/create.ts lines 91-93: getFlag() calls for all 3 flags
src/tickets/create.ts lines 95-137: resolveTicket() calls with try/catch for each flag
src/tickets/create.ts lines 143-151: POST body with conditional spread operators
src/lib/resolve-ticket.ts lines 86-167: resolveTicket() returns { id: string; shortId: string }

answers.answer: Yes. The error handling in create.ts lines 154-172 correctly splits on the em-dash separator (` — `) used by hxFetch's buildErrorMessage (http.ts line 34), parses the JSON body portion, and extracts the `error` field. Falls back to the raw message if JSON parsing fails.
evidence: src/tickets/create.ts lines 157-168: dashIdx = msg.indexOf(' — '); JSON.parse(bodyPart); parsed.error extraction

answers.answer: Yes. TicketDetail in get.ts adds 6 fields matching the server detail endpoint shape (afterTicketId, afterTicket with mode, implementFromTicketId, implementFromTicket with mode, referencedTicketIds, referencedTickets). TicketItem in list.ts adds 4 fields matching the list endpoint shape (afterTicketId, afterTicket without mode, implementFromTicketId, referencedTicketIds).
evidence: src/tickets/get.ts lines 5-38: RelatedTicket type and TicketDetail extension
src/tickets/list.ts lines 5-17: TicketItem type with list-specific afterTicket shape (no mode field)
ticket.md Research Report sections 2.4-2.5: server response shapes for detail and list endpoints

answers.answer: Yes. get.ts lines 80-89 conditionally render Depends on/Implements/References sections only when afterTicket/implementFromTicket/referencedTickets are present. list.ts line 111 computes afterTag only when afterTicket exists. Tickets without relationships display identically to before.
evidence: src/tickets/get.ts lines 80-89: conditional if blocks for each relationship type
src/tickets/list.ts line 111: ternary for afterTag with empty string fallback

answers.answer: One inconsistency found and fixed: commands.md used `--repo` (singular) on the tickets create line but the actual CLI flag is `--repos` (plural). Fixed to `--repos <name1,name2>`. All other documentation surfaces (cli-content.ts, SKILL.md, index.ts, create.ts help) correctly use `--repos`.
Yes. TypeScript compilation passes with zero errors (npx tsc --noEmit). All 51 tests pass (0 fail, 0 skipped). Both gates verified before and after the commands.md fix.
evidence: tsc && node --test dist/**/*.test.js: 51 pass, 0 fail, 0 cancelled, 0 skipped

statement: Code review complete for helix-cli ticket relationship support. All 7 changed files reviewed against the ticket research report, product spec, and implementation plan. One documentation inconsistency found and fixed: commands.md used `--repo` (singular) instead of `--repos` (plural) for the tickets create command. No code bugs, no regressions, no missed requirements. TypeScript compilation clean, all 51 tests pass after fix.
